ticket.description:
Hello,
The app is almost setup.
Could you call us today to finish, there is still some details we would like to handle for DHL. Don:t need to call us if you can fix it on your end.
1- We disable Estimated Delivery At Checkout and still DHL option displays 2 days.
2- the display name of DHL should be "DHL (Pas de douanes pour l'Europe Metropolitaine)" however it remains wolrdwide express.

We need to fix these 2 problems.

------------------
Envoyé de : https://mcsl-s.pluginhive.io/home/settings/automation/setup/17e487bf-49c0-450d-be18-d9783854f44b

comments.body:
Hello Team,

Good Day!

Upon checking, I could observe that the "EXPRESS WORLDWIDE" option you are seeing on checkout is not coming from our app.

This option is being shown from the "DHL Commerce" app you have installed and added on your store.

Please refer to the attached screen recording for further explanation on both your cases.

Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:

comments.body:
Hello Team,

Good Day!

Upon checking, we could observe that you are currently using the **Weight Based Packing Method** with the app.

When using this, the app considers only the weight of the products for rates calculation.

For the app to consider the weight and dimensions of the products for rates calculation, you should be using either the **Box Packing Method** or **Weight and Volume Based Packing Method** with the app.
**Packing Methods** - https://www.pluginhive.com/knowledge-base/setting-up-shopify-multi-carrier-shipping-label-app/#package-methods

You can navigate to **App Menu > Settings > Shipping > Packaging** to set up the app as per your requirement.

Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Team,
 
Good Day!
 
Upon checking, we could observe that you are currently using the Weight Based Packing Method with the app. 
 
When using this, the app considers only the weight of the products for rates calculation.
 
For the app to consider the weight and dimensions of the products for rates calculation, you should be using either the Box Packing Method or Weight and Volume Based Packing Method with the app.
Packing Methods - https://www.pluginhive.com/knowledge-base/setting-up-shopify-multi-carrier-shipping-label-app/#package-methods
 
You can navigate to App Menu > Settings > Shipping > Packaging to set up the app as per your requirement.
 
Let us know if you have any further queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Team,

Good Day!

As we are not aware of your packaging requirements, we cannot set up the app for your usage.
The setup requires information like - will app the products be packed individually, or multiple products can be packed in a single package? If so, which package sizes should be used?
Are there different package sizes that the app should use based on the products in the order? If so, what are the box sizes that you use on a regular basis.

As we do not have this information, you as the store owner, will have to set up the app as per your requirements in which we can assist over a Zoom call, if needed.

If you would like to connect over a Zoom call to discuss your requirements about packaging and take our assistance with the setup, you can **CLICK HERE** to schedule the call as per your availability.

Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:

The setup requires information like - will app the products be packed individually, or multiple products can be packed in a single package? If so, which package sizes should be used? 
Are there different package sizes that the app should use based on the products in the order? If so, what are the box sizes that you use on a regular basis.
 
As we do not have this information, you as the store owner, will have to set up the app as per your requirements in which we can assist over a Zoom call, if needed.
 
If you would like to connect over a Zoom call to discuss your requirements about packaging and take our assistance with the setup, you can CLICK HERE to schedule the call as per your availability.
 
Let us know if you have any further queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Team,

Thank you for your response.

To make the Packaging changes in the PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label app taking the PH Ship Rate and Track for FedEx app as reference, we would require your **explicit confirmation and permission** that we can make the changes from our end in the app on your store.

Additionally, we have also updated the product dimensions in the PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label app taking the PH Ship Rate and Track for FedEx app product data as reference.

You can cross-check few products in the app and let us know if you have any queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Team,
 
Thank you for your response.
 
To make the Packaging changes in the PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label app taking the PH Ship Rate and Track for FedEx app as reference, we would require your explicit confirmation and permission that we can make the changes from our end in the app on your store.
 
Additionally, we have also updated the product dimensions in the PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label app taking the PH Ship Rate and Track for FedEx app product data as reference.
 
You can cross-check few products in the app and let us know if you have any queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Dear Touseef,



Thank you for your feedback.

Here our explicit confirmation and permission. We would like your help to set up the PH MultiCarrier Shipping Label app exactly as the PH Ship Rate & Track for FedEx app. We already exported the CSV files and entered our products dimensions. The only thing we need is the PH MultiCarrier Shipping Label app to handle the order like the PH Ship Rate & Track for FedEx app.

For example:

-1 product 1x1x1 cm 0.9 Kg will be the reflect of 0.9kg for the volumic price

-2 product 1x1x1 cm 1.8Kg will be the reflect of 1.8kg for the volumic price

-1 product 40x23x20 cm 1.0Kg will be the reflect of 40x23x20 for the volumic price

-1 product 40x23x20 cm 0.4Kg & 1 product 13x26x17 cm 0.8Kg will be the reflect of the combined volumic price.



Sincerely,

Jap'On Demande

Tokyo, Bunkyo

www.japondemande.com
 ![](https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/mail-sig/AIorK4zeYwMpRvmW4lNTBbVT-_7dqrksrdW7hfm6uvHTK7I9ShkAO9G5wjdNv-nedgoWLQ-GcWaQ-4ydf3e6)

comments.body:
Hi Team,

Thank you for sharing the detailed information and examples.

To ensure the setup behaves exactly as required and matches your current PH Ship Rate & Track for FedEx configuration, we recommend going through this together on a call. This will help us set up the packaging and volumetric calculations correctly and ensure everything works smoothly for your use case.

Please note that while the packaging settings are similar in both apps, there are slight differences, so configuring this over a call would be the best approach.
Kindly let me know your availability, or you can book a call at your convenience using the link below:
**CLICK HERE**

Looking forward to assisting you.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hi Team,
 
Thank you for sharing the detailed information and examples.
 
To ensure the setup behaves exactly as required and matches your current PH Ship Rate & Track for FedEx configuration, we recommend going through this together on a call. This will help us set up the packaging and volumetric calculations correctly and ensure everything works smoothly for your use case.
 
Please note that while the packaging settings are similar in both apps, there are slight differences, so configuring this over a call would be the best approach.
Kindly let me know your availability, or you can book a call at your convenience using the link below:
CLICK HERE
 
Looking forward to assisting you.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Rates are not matching in the app for both DHL Express and FedEx.


- For FedEx, we have identified the case that the insurance surcharge is being added in the rates from API. Team is checking for a resolution.
- For DHL, rates from the app is **€54.98** for **EXPRESS WORLDWIDE** service, whereas on the customer account which the same details, it is **JPY 8,275 (~€45.01)**
The app is requesting for an Export Declaration Surcharge of **JPY1800 (~€9.81)** from DHL based on the order total. This surcharge is requested for order total more than 25000.
Customer is shipping from Japan and for the order 292011, the order value is €254.01 (~46615.89 JPY). However, this is not applicable when shipping from Japan as the rates in customer account as without this surcharge.
Next step is to confirm with customer what is the Export Declaration amount limit for shipping from Japan (like 25K added currently) so that the value can be updated on our end for rates to be shown correctly.

  • For FedEx, we have identified the case that the insurance surcharge is being added in the rates from API. Team is checking for a resolution.

    The app is requesting for an Export Declaration Surcharge of JPY1800 (~€9.81) from DHL based on the order total. This surcharge is requested for order total more than 25000.
    Customer is shipping from Japan and for the order 292011, the order value is €254.01 (~46615.89 JPY). However, this is not applicable when shipping from Japan as the rates in customer account as without this surcharge.
    Next step is to confirm with customer what is the Export Declaration amount limit for shipping from Japan (like 25K added currently) so that the value can be updated on our end for rates to be shown correctly.
comments.plain_body:
Rates are not matching in the app for both DHL Express and FedEx.
 
  For FedEx, we have identified the case that the insurance surcharge is being added in the rates from API. Team is checking for a resolution.  For DHL, rates from the app is €54.98 for EXPRESS WORLDWIDE service, whereas on the customer account which the same details, it is JPY 8,275 (~€45.01)
The app is requesting for an Export Declaration Surcharge of JPY1800 (~€9.81) from DHL based on the order total. This surcharge is requested for order total more than 25000.
Customer is shipping from Japan and for the order 292011, the order value is €254.01 (~46615.89 JPY). However, this is not applicable when shipping from Japan as the rates in customer account as without this surcharge.
Next step is to confirm with customer what is the Export Declaration amount limit for shipping from Japan (like 25K added currently) so that the value can be updated on our end for rates to be shown correctly.

comments.body:
Hello Adrien,

Thank you for your time on the call.

I have now checked your case about the rates mismatch from the app for both DHL Express and FedEx.


-

For **DHL Express**, the app is adding an **EXPORT DECLARATION surcharge**, which is applied for **high value shipments**. The **default setup in the app uses a shipment value of 25000 (in local currency)** for this surcharge to be added.

This value **may not be accurate when shipping from Japan**. Could you please **reach out to the DHL team to confirm the correct threshold value** above which this surcharge should apply for shipments from Japan?

Once confirmed, **we will update the value in the app** so that the **rates are reflected correctly** for your DHL Express shipments.


- For FedEx, the rates are not matching as Insurance Surcharge is being added by FedEx for International Shipments. We have contacted FedEx to get more information on this case and we will enhance the app to correctly show the rates from the app.

Looking forward to your response about the DHL EXPORT DECLARATION shipment value.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Adrien,
 
Thank you for your time on the call.
 
I have now checked your case about the rates mismatch from the app for both DHL Express and FedEx.
 

  • For DHL Express, the app is adding an EXPORT DECLARATION surcharge, which is applied for high value shipments. The default setup in the app uses a shipment value of 25000 (in local currency) for this surcharge to be added.

    This value may not be accurate when shipping from Japan. Could you please reach out to the DHL team to confirm the correct threshold value above which this surcharge should apply for shipments from Japan?

    Once confirmed, we will update the value in the app so that the rates are reflected correctly for your DHL Express shipments.
     

  • For FedEx, the rates are not matching as Insurance Surcharge is being added by FedEx for International Shipments. We have contacted FedEx to get more information on this case and we will enhance the app to correctly show the rates from the app.
 
Looking forward to your response about the DHL EXPORT DECLARATION shipment value.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Adrien,

Thank you for your response.

For the case with DHL Express, disabling the EXPORT DECLARATION value check will not be possible as this may work for your case, however, it may not be a feasible solution for all scenarios and countries.

Could you please **reach out to the DHL team to confirm the correct threshold value** above which this surcharge should apply for shipments from Japan?
Once confirmed, **we will update the value in the app** so that the **rates are reflected correctly** for your DHL Express shipments.

Awaiting your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Adrien,

I believe I was not clear in my last response.

The EXPORT DECLARATION value check in the app with DHL Express is not limited to your store. This is a setup used by all the merchants who use the app with DHL Express across the globe.

Disabling this completely may cause challenges for customers who are using the app from other countries and shipping from that country (e.g., US or UK).

We can check the case with DHL team from our end on your behalf. Could you please share your DHL Account Representative contact (email id) with us? We will reach out to them and check the case further.

Awaiting your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1775125788247069

DHL rates mismatch case
Japan customer added his DHL and FedEx account with the app.
**Summary of the case - Example Order 292011**
For DHL, rates from the app is **€54.98** for **EXPRESS WORLDWIDE** service, whereas on the customer account which the same details, it is **JPY 8,275 (~€45.01)**
The app is requesting for an Export Declaration Surcharge of **JPY1800 (~€9.81)** from DHL based on the order total. This surcharge is requested for order total more than 25000.
Customer is shipping from Japan and for the order 292011, the order value is €254.01 (~46615.89 JPY). However, this is not applicable when shipping from Japan as the rates in customer account as without this surcharge.

Reached out to DHL Japan team with help of customer on https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/382091

DHL team replied the following -
************
_I would like to clarify an important point regarding the rate mismatch:_
_*Export Declaration (WO) charges are generally not applied for exports from Japan._
_*Therefore, we kindly request that your system does not include or request the WO surcharge when retrieving rates from DHL._
************

Could you please check the case and let us know if the suggested change by DHL team can be added in the app?

URL - bbbb09-31.myshopify.com
Account ID - 1ad7b600-4ad8-4d4a-9001-ce443a2d12e0

comments.body:
Hello Adrien,

Thank you for your patience.

After discussing your case with the DHL team, we will be updating the app as per directions from the DHL team for your case.

This enhancement will be made available in the upcoming release of the app.

We will update you as soon as this enhancement is made available so that you can test the rates case further.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Adrien,

Thank you for the followup.

We have extended the trial for the app on your store by 14 days.

We will update you as soon as the enhancement for DHL rates mismatch case is addressed.

Additionally, I would like to update that the FedEx rates mismatch case has now been addressed in the app. You can try checking for FedEx rates and let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Adrien,
 
Thank you for the followup.
 
We have extended the trial for the app on your store by 14 days.
 
We will update you as soon as the enhancement for DHL rates mismatch case is addressed.
 
Additionally, I would like to update that the FedEx rates mismatch case has now been addressed in the app. You can try checking for FedEx rates and let us know if you have any further queries.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
